scopes_skipped.reason: livespec has no live system to probe (no DB / no edge functions); inspection is static-only

inspection.inspection_method: static — dev-tooling/implementation/refresh_gaps.py walked the repo via hardcoded gap-detection predicates

gaps.title: plan.py automation missing — plan skill is currently manual-fallback-only
gaps.expected: dev-tooling/implementation/plan.py exists and reads implementation-gaps/current.json, surfaces untracked gaps to the user, calls `bd create` (with `--label gap-id:<id>`) for confirmed gaps, establishes `bd dep add` edges per the depends_on field, and commits the auto-exported .beads/issues.jsonl as a planning commit.
gaps.observed: dev-tooling/implementation/plan.py does not exist. The plan SKILL.md describes the manual fallback workflow.
gaps.fix_hint: Author dev-tooling/implementation/plan.py with the bd noninteractive command repertoire. Surface gaps to user before creation; honour depends_on as `bd dep add` edges. Re-run check-gap-tracking after each batch.

gaps.title: implement.py automation missing — implement skill is currently manual-fallback-only
gaps.expected: dev-tooling/implementation/implement.py exists. Pulls a leaf-level issue from `bd ready --json`, drives a Red-Green code cycle (delegating to the user / agent for the actual code edits), re-runs refresh-gaps, verifies the gap-id is gone from current.json, then calls `bd close --resolution fix --note '<audit fields>'` per non-functional-requirements.md §Constraints close-with-audit-fields rule.
gaps.observed: dev-tooling/implementation/implement.py does not exist. The implement SKILL.md describes the manual fallback workflow (run by hand).
gaps.fix_hint: Author dev-tooling/implementation/implement.py with `bd ready --json` polling, audit-field formatting for `bd close --note`, and a hard refusal to close any issue whose gap-id still appears in current.json after a fresh refresh-gaps run.

gaps.title: Three SKILL.md files are minimal stubs — full operational prose deferred
gaps.expected: Each of refresh-gaps / plan / implement SKILL.md contains full operational prose covering the entire workflow the skill drives, modelled on Open Brain's openbrain/skills/{update-specification-drift,plan,implement}/SKILL.md (300-500 lines each), with the manual-fallback section either removed or scoped to legitimate degraded-mode situations rather than 'Python automation does not exist yet'.
gaps.observed: At least one SKILL.md file under .claude/skills/livespec-implementation-beads:* still contains the 'Manual fallback (current state)' section, indicating the stub-prose state has not yet been retired.
gaps.fix_hint: Once gap-0001 / gap-0002 / gap-0003 are closed and the Python automation exists, expand each SKILL.md to reference the now-existing entry points and remove the 'Manual fallback (current state)' sections.

gaps.title: .beads/config.yaml is git-tracked — Open Brain convention is to gitignore it (per-clone host URL drift)
gaps.expected: Per Open Brain's setup-beads.sh comments and OB's top-level .gitignore: .beads/config.yaml is gitignored because the dolt remote URL it embeds is host-specific (HTTPS on Linux/VPS, SSH on macOS), causing per-machine diff thrash if tracked. Livespec's spec defers to OB conventions (canonical pattern source) so the same gitignore entry applies.
gaps.observed: .beads/config.yaml is git-tracked; bd init committed it as part of the first beads-init commit on the bootstrap branch. No `.beads/config.yaml` line in the top-level .gitignore.
evidence: dev-tooling/implementation/setup-beads.sh — re-asserts every required value on each run, so a per-clone config.yaml is fully reproducible
gaps.fix_hint: Add `.beads/config.yaml` to top-level .gitignore and `git rm --cached .beads/config.yaml`. Subsequent `setup-beads` runs re-assert the required values, so the per-clone file regenerates automatically.
